nodes.label: P4.6 — noise robustness (A7): the substrate axis
nodes.rationale: An honest negative: under eval-time multiplicative weight noise on a clean-trained model, OURS is the LEAST robust racer (0.754 retention vs BP 0.875) — refuting the plan's optimistic 'forward-only is noise-robust' expectation for this narrow noise model; the decisive train-with-noise test is routed to a later phase.

nodes.label: A7 — eval-time weight-noise robustness: an honest negative
nodes.rationale: Under eval-time multiplicative Gaussian weight noise on a clean-trained model, OURS is the LEAST robust of three racers (retention 0.754 at sigma=0.4 vs BP's 0.875) — refutes the plan's optimistic hypothesis that forward-only is hardware-noise-robust, but the scope is narrow: this is not the substrate's real regime (train-with-noise / hardware-aware training).

nodes.label: The layernorm robustness<->noise-sensitivity tradeoff (A2 win and A7 loss share one cause)
nodes.rationale: Per-sample layernorm gives OURS its nuisance-dimension robustness (A2) by renormalizing magnitude after each matmul, but multiplicative weight noise perturbs activation DIRECTION which layernorm cannot damp — that direction error compounds through 4 layernormed layers plus the all-tap readout, making OURS weight-noise-sensitive; a genuine architectural tradeoff, not a bug.

nodes.label: The decisive noise test is train-with-noise, routed forward
nodes.rationale: The literature's forward-only noise-robustness claim is about learning online WITH analog noise present (hardware-aware training), not eval-time noise on a clean-trained model; this untested regime is routed to a later phase (the analog/PVT realism layer) before any noise-robustness claim is made.
